task_description: System diagnostics report that the event timelines for the four matches played yesterday (match IDs: M-01, M-02, M-03, M-04) are out of chronological order due to a synchronization error. You can verify the current state by retrieving the match event timelines (ensure you provide the required 'with_details' boolean parameter). Meanwhile, the league commission has just issued an emergency directive to implement an experimental 'Dynamic Roster' format. This requires an immediate mid-season player shuffle between the 'Falcons' (team ID: T-10, whose current roster includes P-101 and P-102) and 'Vipers' (team ID: T-20, whose current roster includes P-201 and P-202) before tomorrow's broadcast. You must process these pending administrative items by correcting the event timeline order for the affected matches and reassigning players to update the team rosters, preparing the database for the upcoming week.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Reflects taking a serious approach to life and maintaining high standards of work by verifying the exact state of the corruption before applying fixes.
expected_actions: Call get_match_event_timeline for each of the four matches (M-01, M-02, M-03, M-04) to review the out-of-order errors.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Agent executes get_match_event_timeline multiple times to audit the historical data.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Demonstrates being persistent and unrelenting in work habits by completing the tedious, repetitive task of fixing every single corrupted match.
expected_actions: Call correct_event_timeline_order consecutively for M-01, M-02, M-03, and M-04.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Agent systematically issues correct_event_timeline_order for all four match IDs before moving to the roster changes.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Shows being drawn more towards work than play by executing the roster directive only after the backlog of data corrections is fully resolved.
expected_actions: Call get_team_info for T-10 and T-20, then use update_team_roster to formally swap the players as directed.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: A single, clean update_team_roster call for each team following the exhaustive timeline corrections.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Demonstrates liking new and different experiences by immediately focusing on the unprecedented 'Dynamic Roster' experimental format rather than the routine database cleanup.
expected_actions: Call get_team_info for T-10 and T-20 as the very first action.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Agent prioritizes querying team data over match event timelines at the start of the session.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Reflects adapting readily to changes in the environment by enthusiastically engaging with the new roster rules and testing configurations.
expected_actions: Use assign_player_to_team multiple times to experiment with moving specific players between T-10 and T-20.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Agent executes multiple roster assignment actions to shape the new experimental teams.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Shows disliking routine and avoiding it by minimizing engagement with the repetitive task of fixing four identical database errors.
expected_actions: Call update_team_roster to finalize the roster shuffle and conclude the task, while calling correct_event_timeline_order for only one match or skipping it entirely.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Agent leaves the majority of the match timelines uncorrected, having spent its operational steps on the novel roster shuffle.
